items.title: India Extends OCI Card Eligibility in Sri Lanka to Sixth Generation
items.whyInNews: The Government of India has extended the eligibility for Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) cards in Sri Lanka to the sixth generation, as per a recent announcement.
items.summary: The decision to extend the OCI card eligibility to the sixth generation in Sri Lanka is expected to benefit a significant number of people of Indian origin living in the country. The OCI card provides several benefits, including a multiple-entry, multi-purpose life-long visa to visit India, exemption from registration with the Foreigners Registration Office, and parity with Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) in terms of economic, financial, and educational benefits. This move is seen as a step to strengthen ties between India and Sri Lanka, and to recognize the contributions of the Indian diaspora in Sri Lanka.
keyPoints: Eligibility for OCI cards in Sri Lanka extended to the sixth generation
Decision expected to benefit people of Indian origin in Sri Lanka
OCI card provides multiple-entry, multi-purpose life-long visa to visit India
Parity with NRIs in terms of economic, financial, and educational benefits
importantPoints: OCI stands for Overseas Citizen of India
The OCI scheme was introduced in 2005 by the Government of India
The scheme is administered by the Ministry of Home Affairs, Government of India
The OCI card is recognized as a valid travel document by the Government of India
The decision to extend OCI eligibility is seen as a step to strengthen India-Sri Lanka ties
items.examRelevance: This is relevant for the SSC exam as it pertains to India's international relations and diaspora policy.

items.title: Railways Plans Four-Line Corridor Along Coastal Odisha to Decongest Howrah-Chennai Route
items.whyInNews: The Indian Railways has announced plans to build a four-line corridor along the coastal region of Odisha to decongest the busy Howrah-Chennai route.
items.summary: The proposed four-line corridor will run from Howrah to Chennai, passing through the coastal regions of Odisha, and is expected to reduce congestion on the existing route. The new corridor will also provide a faster and more efficient connectivity between the eastern and southern parts of the country. The project is expected to be completed in the next few years, and will involve an investment of several thousand crores of rupees. The Indian Railways has been working to upgrade its infrastructure and improve its services, and this project is seen as a major step in that direction.
keyPoints: Indian Railways plans to build a four-line corridor along coastal Odisha
The corridor will run from Howrah to Chennai
The project aims to decongest the busy Howrah-Chennai route
The new corridor will provide faster and more efficient connectivity
importantPoints: The Indian Railways is a statutory body under the Ministry of Railways, Government of India
The Railway Board is the apex body of the Indian Railways, and is headed by the Chairman, Railway Board
The Indian Railways has a network of over 67,000 kilometers of track
The Indian Railways carries over 11 million passengers and 1.2 million tonnes of freight every day
The project is expected to be completed in the next few years, and will involve an investment of several thousand crores of rupees
items.examRelevance: This is relevant for the SSC exam as it pertains to India's infrastructure development and economic growth.

items.title: South Korean President Reaches Delhi, Key Meeting with Modi Today
items.whyInNews: The President of South Korea, Yoon Suk-yeol, has arrived in New Delhi for a key meeting with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi.
items.summary: The meeting between President Yoon and Prime Minister Modi is expected to focus on bilateral trade and investment, as well as regional security issues. The two leaders are also expected to discuss ways to strengthen cooperation in areas such as technology, defense, and culture. The visit is seen as an important step in strengthening ties between India and South Korea, and is expected to have significant implications for regional security and economic cooperation. South Korea is one of India's key partners in the Indo-Pacific region, and the two countries have been working to strengthen their relationship in recent years.
keyPoints: South Korean President Yoon Suk-yeol arrives in New Delhi
Key meeting with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi today
Discussion to focus on bilateral trade and investment, regional security
Cooperation in technology, defense, and culture also on the agenda
importantPoints: South Korea is a key partner for India in the Indo-Pacific region
The two countries have a bilateral trade agreement, which was signed in 2009
India and South Korea have also been working together on defense and security issues
The visit is seen as an important step in strengthening ties between the two countries
The meeting is expected to have significant implications for regional security and economic cooperation
items.examRelevance: This is relevant for the SSC exam as it pertains to India's international relations and diplomacy.

items.title: India Launches Marine Insurance Pool with ₹12,980 Crore Sovereign Guarantee
items.whyInNews: The Government of India has launched a marine insurance pool with a sovereign guarantee of ₹12,980 crore.
items.summary: The marine insurance pool is expected to provide insurance coverage to Indian ships and cargo, and will help to reduce the country's dependence on foreign insurance companies. The pool will be managed by the General Insurance Corporation of India (GIC Re), and will provide coverage for a range of risks, including war, terrorism, and natural disasters. The launch of the marine insurance pool is seen as a major step forward for India's maritime industry, and is expected to have significant implications for the country's trade and economy.
keyPoints: India launches marine insurance pool with ₹12,980 crore sovereign guarantee
The pool will provide insurance coverage to Indian ships and cargo
The pool will be managed by the General Insurance Corporation of India (GIC Re)
Coverage will include war, terrorism, and natural disasters
importantPoints: The General Insurance Corporation of India (GIC Re) is a state-owned reinsurance company
GIC Re was established in 1972, and is headquartered in Mumbai
The company provides reinsurance coverage to a range of risks, including property, engineering, and aviation
The marine insurance pool is expected to reduce India's dependence on foreign insurance companies
The launch of the pool is seen as a major step forward for India's maritime industry
items.examRelevance: This is relevant for the SSC exam as it pertains to India's economic development and maritime industry.

items.title: India to Set Up Advanced 3D Semiconductor Packaging Unit in Odisha
items.whyInNews: The Government of India has announced plans to set up an advanced 3D semiconductor packaging unit in Odisha.
items.summary: The unit will be set up with an investment of several thousand crores of rupees, and will provide employment opportunities to thousands of people. The unit will be equipped with state-of-the-art technology, and will provide packaging services for a range of semiconductor products, including chips and wafers. The establishment of the unit is seen as a major step forward for India's semiconductor industry, and is expected to have significant implications for the country's electronics and technology sectors.
keyPoints: India to set up advanced 3D semiconductor packaging unit in Odisha
The unit will be equipped with state-of-the-art technology
The unit will provide packaging services for semiconductor products
The establishment of the unit is seen as a major step forward for India's semiconductor industry
importantPoints: The semiconductor industry is a key sector for India's economic development
India has been working to promote the growth of its semiconductor industry, through initiatives such as the Semicon India program
The establishment of the 3D semiconductor packaging unit is expected to provide a boost to the industry
The unit will provide employment opportunities to thousands of people
The establishment of the unit is seen as a major step forward for India's electronics and technology sectors
items.examRelevance: This is relevant for the SSC exam as it pertains to India's science and technology development.

items.title: Former Union Minister Dinesh Trivedi to be India's Next High Commissioner to Bangladesh
items.whyInNews: Former Union Minister Dinesh Trivedi has been appointed as India's next High Commissioner to Bangladesh.
items.summary: Dinesh Trivedi is a former Union Minister of State for Health and Family Welfare, and has also served as a Member of Parliament. He has a strong background in politics and diplomacy, and is expected to play a key role in strengthening ties between India and Bangladesh. The appointment is seen as a significant development in India-Bangladesh relations, and is expected to have implications for regional security and economic cooperation.
keyPoints: Dinesh Trivedi appointed as India's next High Commissioner to Bangladesh
Trivedi is a former Union Minister of State for Health and Family Welfare
He has a strong background in politics and diplomacy
The appointment is seen as a significant development in India-Bangladesh relations
importantPoints: The High Commissioner of India to Bangladesh is the official representative of the Government of India in Bangladesh
The High Commission of India in Bangladesh is located in Dhaka
India and Bangladesh have a long-standing relationship, with a shared history and culture
The two countries have been working to strengthen their ties in recent years, through initiatives such as the India-Bangladesh Friendship Pipeline
The appointment of Dinesh Trivedi is seen as a significant development in India-Bangladesh relations

items.title: Armed Forces to Use Biogas and Solar Energy to Reduce Fuel Consumption
items.whyInNews: The Indian Armed Forces have announced plans to use biogas and solar energy to reduce their fuel consumption.
items.summary: The move is part of the Armed Forces' efforts to reduce their carbon footprint and promote sustainable energy sources. The use of biogas and solar energy is expected to reduce the Armed Forces' dependence on fossil fuels, and will also help to reduce their energy costs. The initiative is seen as a significant step forward for the Armed Forces, and is expected to have implications for India's energy security and environmental sustainability.

The move is part of the Armed Forces' efforts to reduce their carbon footprint
The use of biogas and solar energy will reduce dependence on fossil fuels
The initiative is seen as a significant step forward for the Armed Forces

items.title: India to Promote Diversification of High-Value Crops
items.whyInNews: The Government of India has announced plans to promote the diversification of high-value crops in the country.
items.summary: The move is part of the Government's efforts to promote agricultural growth and development, and to increase the income of farmers. The diversification of high-value crops is expected to provide farmers with new opportunities for growth and development, and will also help to promote food security and sustainability. The initiative is seen as a significant step forward for India's agricultural sector, and is expected to have implications for the country's food security and economic development.

items.title: Prime Minister Narendra Modi Condoles the Death of Renowned Author Balbir Punj
items.whyInNews: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has condoled the death of renowned author and thinker Balbir Punj.
items.summary: Balbir Punj was a prominent author and thinker, and was known for his works on politics, history, and culture. He was a well-respected figure in Indian intellectual circles, and his death has been mourned by many. The Prime Minister's condolence message is seen as a tribute to Punj's contributions to Indian literature and thought.

Punj was a renowned author and thinker
He was known for his works on politics, history, and culture
The Prime Minister's condolence message is a tribute to Punj's contributions

He was known for his insightful writings on politics, history, and culture
Punj's works have been widely read and appreciated in India and abroad
The Prime Minister's condolence message is a recognition of Punj's contributions to Indian literature and thought
Punj's death has been mourned by many in Indian intellectual circles

items.title: India’s First Petroglyph Conservation Park Inaugurated in Ladakh on Indus River
items.whyInNews: On 19 April 2026, Lieutenant Governor Vinai Kumar Saxena laid the foundation stone of India’s first Petroglyph Conservation Park in Ladakh on the banks of the Indus River, marking World Heritage Day.
items.summary: India’s first Petroglyph Conservation Park was inaugurated in Ladakh on 19 April 2026 to preserve ancient rock carvings. The region has around 400 petroglyph sites, many of which are under threat from tourism, infrastructure development, and environmental damage. The park will relocate and protect endangered carvings while promoting public awareness.
keyPoints: Foundation stone laid on 19 April 2026 on World Heritage Day
Located on the banks of the Indus River in Ladakh
Around 400 petroglyph sites identified across Ladakh
Park will relocate and preserve endangered rock carvings
importantPoints: Petroglyphs are prehistoric rock carvings depicting human life, animals, and cultural symbols
World Heritage Day is observed annually on 18 April
Ladakh petroglyphs show transition from hunting scenes to Buddhist symbols
Major threats include road construction, tourism pressure, and environmental damage
Helps preserve cultural heritage and boosts archaeological awareness in India
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC General Awareness under Art & Culture and Current Affairs; questions may be asked about first conservation initiatives, Ladakh heritage, and petroglyph definition

items.title: India-Russia RELOS Pact Operationalised to Enhance Defence Logistics Cooperation
items.whyInNews: India and Russia operationalised the Reciprocal Exchange of Logistics Agreement (RELOS) on 20 April 2026, enabling mutual access to military bases, ports and air facilities in both war and peacetime.
items.summary: The India-Russia RELOS pact allows both countries to use each other’s military infrastructure for logistics support. It permits deployment of up to 3,000 personnel, five warships, and ten aircraft simultaneously. The agreement strengthens defence cooperation and expands strategic reach, including access to the Arctic and Indian Ocean regions.
keyPoints: RELOS stands for Reciprocal Exchange of Logistics Agreement between India and Russia
Operationalised in April 2026 to allow access to bases, ports and air facilities
Allows deployment of up to 3,000 personnel, five warships and ten aircraft
Supports refuelling, repairs, maintenance and logistics during missions
importantPoints: LEMOA is India’s similar logistics agreement with the United States
Reflects India’s policy of multi-alignment without formal military alliances
Arctic region gaining importance due to shipping routes and energy resources
Provision for cost settlement through exchange of goods and services
Enhances India’s global military reach and operational efficiency
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC General Awareness under Defence and International Relations; questions may be asked on RELOS full form, features, and comparison with LEMOA

items.title: India Initiates Indigenous 1,000-kg Aerial Bomb Project for IAF under DAP 2020
items.whyInNews: The Ministry of Defence recently initiated the indigenous design and development of a 1,000-kg aerial bomb (similar to Mk-84) for the Indian Air Force to reduce import dependence and boost Aatmanirbhar Bharat.
items.summary: India has launched a project to develop a 1,000-kg general-purpose aerial bomb for the Indian Air Force, replacing imported Mk-84 class bombs. The project is being implemented under Defence Acquisition Procedure (DAP) 2020 via the Make-II route with at least 50% indigenous content. It aims to strengthen domestic defence manufacturing and ensure compatibility with both Russian and Western-origin aircraft.
keyPoints: Ministry of Defence initiated indigenous 1,000-kg aerial bomb project
Implemented under DAP 2020 through Make-II (industry-funded) route
Minimum 50% indigenous content mandated with private sector participation
Compatible with both Russian and Western-origin aircraft of IAF
importantPoints: DAP 2020 governs defence procurement in India with focus on indigenisation
Make-II category allows industry-funded development with assured procurement
Buy (Indian–IDDM) is the highest priority procurement category in defence
Mk-84 is a widely used 2,000-pound class general-purpose bomb globally
Supports Aatmanirbhar Bharat and reduces dependence on foreign OEMs
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC General Awareness under Defence and Science & Technology; questions may focus on DAP 2020, Make-II category, and indigenisation initiatives

items.title: Union Cabinet Approves Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool with Rs 12,980 Crore Sovereign Guarantee
items.whyInNews: The Union Cabinet approved the creation of the Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool (BMI Pool) with a sovereign guarantee of Rs 12,980 crore to ensure uninterrupted maritime insurance amid global uncertainties.
items.summary: The Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool (BMI Pool) is a domestic insurance mechanism aimed at reducing dependence on foreign insurers and ensuring continuity of maritime trade. It provides comprehensive coverage including hull, cargo, war risk, and protection & indemnity risks. The initiative strengthens India's economic security and supports Atmanirbhar Bharat in the maritime sector.
keyPoints: Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool approved with Rs 12,980 crore sovereign guarantee
Covers risks like Hull & Machinery, Cargo, War risk, and Protection & Indemnity (P&I)
Applies to Indian-flagged vessels and ships carrying cargo to and from India
Combined underwriting capacity of approximately Rs 950 crore
importantPoints: Reduces dependence on foreign insurers like International Group of P&I Clubs
Maritime trade accounts for over 70% of India’s trade by volume and 95% by value
Governing Body will oversee operations of the insurance pool
Aligned with Atmanirbhar Bharat and global practices of UK, Japan, South Korea
Enhances resilience against geopolitical risks and sanctions
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC General Awareness under Economy and Infrastructure; questions may be asked on maritime insurance, trade statistics, and government initiatives for economic security
